summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on June 27, 2023. Key actions included accepting a firefighter's resignation, approving zoning text amendments (Resolution 15), approving the collective bargaining agreement with the police association for 2024-2026, and appointing Dennis Pruitt as a BZA alternate. A resident spoke about media outreach regarding the governor's unfulfilled promise related to township funding, and the board also addressed sign permit refunds and planned future zoning discussions.
decisions: Approved payment of bills for June 13-26, 2023
Accepted resignation of part-time firefighter EMT Elijah Mann
Approved reimbursement of $200 to Lisa Cannon for BZA application no longer needed
Passed Resolution 15 for zoning text amendments including barns/storage structures, minor administrative relief of 10%, and related changes
Adjourned into executive session at 7:12 PM to discuss employment, discipline, compensation, and union negotiations
Approved collective bargaining agreement with Goshen Township Police Association for 2024-2026 (CERB case 2023 MED 030215)
Appointed Dennis Pruitt as alternate on BZA with term ending June 27, 2025
Approved motion to support Lions Club health fair using administration building parking lot
Approved refund of $500 zoning sign permit to One Stop Signs for Goshen Community Church sign face change
action_items: Administrator Rhodes to send four priority zoning topics (zone change process, additional ag zoning, small solar/wind farms, raising small livestock) to consultant for proposed text amendments
Administrator Rhodes to work with Lions Club/Dave Curie to set up health fair date and logistics
Administrator Rhodes to process $500 refund to One Stop Signs (or Goshen Community Church depending on receipt) and apologize for misunderstanding
Administrator Rhodes to submit Resolution 15 zoning text amendments to the county to begin the approval process
Zoning topics to be discussed at regular meetings initially, with special meetings if needed
key_amounts: $200 reimbursement to Lisa Cannon for BZA application
$500 refund for zoning sign permit to One Stop Signs/Goshen Community Church
Resident mentioned potential to turn $2 million into $6 million with matching grant funds from governor's promise
notable_topics: Governor's unfulfilled promise to Goshen Township - resident media outreach on radio (Brian Thomas show/WLW)
Zoning text amendments (Resolution 15) - barns/storage structures, 10% administrative relief
Future zoning priorities: zone change process, additional ag zoning, small solar/wind farms, small livestock
Collective bargaining agreement with police association 2024-2026
BZA alternate appointment of Dennis Pruitt
Lions Club health fair with blood donor and mammogram vans
Goshen Community Church sign permit refund dispute
Resignation of firefighter EMT Elijah Mann to Anderson Township
notes: Executive session lasted from approximately 7:12 PM to 8:55 PM. Dave Mathis appeared on the Brian Thomas radio show and spoke with Cincinnati Enquirer reporter about the governor's unfulfilled funding promise to Goshen Township. The board discussed the distinction between sign face changes (no fee required) and structural sign changes ($500 fee) under the zoning code. Zoning text amendments had been discussed across multiple prior meetings including a special meeting the prior week.
title: BOT Meeting 6-27-23
